children.name: Accent colors are used on components and interaction elements only.
children.characters: Accent colors are used on components and interaction elements only.

children.name: Neutral colors are used for non-component text and content only.
children.characters: Neutral colors are used for non-component text and content only.

children.name: Extended colors are used for subject-specific branding and learning categorization throughout the platform.
children.characters: Extended colors are used for subject-specific branding and learning categorization throughout the platform.

children.name: Color roles dictate where each color should be used within your interface, ensuring consistency and coherence throughout your design.
children.characters: Color roles dictate where each color should be used within your interface, ensuring consistency and coherence throughout your design.

4712:26492.name: Pointer
4712:26492.description:
Pointer components are used to annotate your designs.
You might also refer to them as a "Callout" or "Annotation"

children.name: Using sm for padding and md for vertical/horizontal spacing in the same component is valid. It is rare/not ideal to mix different semantic sizes for the same token type (e.g., pad-x-sm + pad-y-md).
children.characters:
Using sm for padding and md for vertical/horizontal spacing in the same component is valid.
It is rare/not ideal to mix different semantic sizes for the same token type (e.g., pad-x-sm + pad-y-md).

children.name: Form <-> Specific Use Cases Gap XS Token: This token is reserved for tight spacing where elements are closely related. Its primary use is for the space between a form label and its input field (e.g., a text field or dropdown menu).

Core Spacing Principles for Elements The primary spacing tokens are tied to the typographic hierarchy. Use the following as your default guide: Headline Text: Use element-gap-lg and element-x/y-lg Title Text: Use element-gap-md and element-x/y-md Body Text: Use element-gap-smand element-x/y-sm However, mixing tokens is still allowed (see mixing Tokens) if absolutely necessary
children.characters:
Core Spacing Principles for Elements
The primary spacing tokens are tied to the typographic hierarchy. Use the following as your default guide:
Headline Text: Use element-gap-lg and element-x/y-lg
Title Text: Use element-gap-md and element-x/y-md
Body Text: Use element-gap-smand element-x/y-sm
However, mixing tokens is still allowed (see mixing Tokens) if absolutely necessary

174:16602.name: layout-blocks/base
174:16602.description:
⇄ Base Swap Block
↑ Swap me with another ◇ component instance or ↓ Vertical / -> Horizontal Layout Blocks
🛈 Tips: To swap this instance to a new one:
- Open Assets tab or press Shift + i to search for component.
- Press & Hold: Ctrl + Alt then ⛶ Drag & Drop component instance to this base block to quick replace

174:16604.name: swap-freesize
174:16604.description: This instance used to swap block and also resize block

3:114.name: Display/Display 2
3:114.description: Traditional heading elements are designed to work best in the meat of your page content. When you need a heading to stand out, consider using a display heading—a larger, slightly more opinionated heading style.
